_note: Hand-authored diffense review pack for Gurio/brr PR #64. NOT a runner emission. Authored 2026-05-29, extended 2026-05-31 (pass 10: entry-stat distributions, invariants, data-trace, kb axes) to pressure-test the card schema before it is locked. Companion render + findings: kb/diffense-prototype-pr64.md. Runtime home would be .brr/diffense/<task-id>/pack.json (keyed by task, not PR — brr publishes a branch; see #68).

pr.title: fix: poll GitHub PR review comments for @-mention triggers

identity.label: PR #64 — review-comment fix, gate refactor, cheaper polling

lore.descriptive: Inline PR review-comment @-mentions now actually reach the gate (they were invisible — those comments live on /pulls/comments, not /issues/comments), replies land in-thread, and steady-state polling gets cheaper via conditional requests. Three arcs braided into one PR.

arcs.what: inline review-comment round-trip: poll -> task -> in-thread reply
monolithic github.py (1052 lines) -> 12-module package, brnrd-reusable core split out

shape.risk_pointer: 2 concerns (1 med: the seen-id cap) + 1 follow-up — read next

entry_stats.label: public surface
entry_stats.caption: preserved verbatim — __all__ pinned across the 12-module split

dots.title: seen-set stays bounded — threatened on very busy PRs

entry_stats.label: steady-state REST cost
entry_stats.caption: conditional 304s are free — a quiet repo polls at ~0 budget

entry_stats.label: tests on new paths
entry_stats.caption: +6: transport 304, cursor threading, in-thread reply, review-comment event

entry_stats.label: data-shape delta
entry_stats.caption: 1 new event kind, 3 new cursor fields

secondary_stats._demoted: raw size — tells you nothing about what to do

cards.headline: On a very busy PR, a rare edit to an already-handled review comment could re-trigger it — each cursor keeps only the newest 500 ids and forgets the oldest.

lore.descriptive: Every poller bounds its seen-id set with sorted(seen)[-_SEEN_CAP:] (_SEEN_CAP=500), keeping the 500 highest (newest) ids and evicting the lowest (oldest). It appears at 7 sites across the file, one per cursor.

between: bounded cursor state (a cap on the seen set)

cards.honest_nuance: The risk is narrower than 'any handled comment re-fires': the `since` cursor advances to latest_seen, so an evicted id only re-fires if that old comment is *edited* after eviction (bumping its updated_at back into the window) on a PR busy enough to have pushed >500 ids past it. The since-cursor + seen-set is belt-and-suspenders for the common case.
cards.proposed_resolution: Either raise the cap for the review-comment cursors specifically, or switch from a bounded id-set to a timestamp-window dedup keyed on updated_at, or scope the seen-set per PR. Flagging so you can decide whether it is worth touching here or filing.

stats.blast_radius: review-comment + review dedup on very busy PRs; no other card depends on it

provenance.commit_headline: fix: poll GitHub PR review comments for @-mention triggers

cards.headline: A reviewer who Approves with a written summary but no inline comments is invisible to this OSS gate — by design; the managed brnrd webhook is what catches it.

identity.label: gap: summary-only reviews (Approve with body, no line comments)

lore.descriptive: Review *summary* bodies are discovered only via the parent of a freshly-seen line comment. A reviewer who Approves with a written summary and zero inline comments produces a pull_request_review that neither /issues/comments nor /pulls/comments surfaces, so this OSS gate cannot see it without per-PR /reviews scans that would blow the REST budget on busy repos. Left to the brnrd webhook path.

between: poll-based discovery within the REST budget

cards.proposed_resolution: No action in OSS; the managed brnrd App receives pull_request_review webhooks directly and closes the gap with zero polling cost. Surfaced so the asymmetry is not a surprise to a reviewer of this PR.

stats.blast_radius: OSS gate review-summary coverage only; documented as intended

provenance.commit_headline: docs(kb): file the GitHub gate vs brnrd App design + cross-links

cards.headline: The near-future step that would make this feel native: a +1 reaction as a one-tap approve, so a user needn't type '@brr-bot approve'.

lore.descriptive: This PR makes @-mention review comments actionable. The near-future work that would make the loop feel native is reactions-as-signal: a +1 reaction as a lightweight approve gesture for the coming permission-prompt UX, so a user need not type '@brr-bot approve'.

between: this PR's scope (make review comments trigger)

cards.proposed_resolution: Implement brnrd-side first (its webhook path gets issue_comment reactions for free; the cost asymmetry favours it). Filed, not done here.

stats.blast_radius: future permission-prompt UX; nothing in this PR depends on it

provenance.commit_headline: docs(kb): file the GitHub gate vs brnrd App design + cross-links

identity.label: An @-mention on an inline diff comment becomes a task and an in-thread reply

lore.descriptive: The headline fix, end to end: inline review comments live on /pulls/comments, not /issues/comments, so before this PR a mention on a diff line was invisible to the gate. Now it is polled, normalised, turned into a task, and answered in the same review thread.

story.setup: A reviewer leaves an inline comment on a diff line of an open PR, mentioning the configured trigger (e.g. @brr).
story.action: The mention poller's review-comment pass reads /pulls/comments, filters for the mention, extracts pr_number/path/line, and emits a pr-review-comment event carrying github_path + github_line + branch_target.
story.outcome: The daemon builds a task scoped to that hunk; on delivery the response is posted in-thread via /pulls/{n}/comments/{cid}/replies with a quote-pointer preface, not as an orphaned top-level comment.

cards.datum: an @-mention on an inline diff line

stages.label: review comment
stages.note: lives on /pulls/comments — invisible to the gate before this PR

stages.label: poll + filter
stages.datum: kept iff mention ∧ unseen ∧ not self-authored

stages.label: task
stages.datum: task scoped to the hunk (path + line + branch_target)

zoom.summary: reviewer mentions @brr on a diff line -> brr replies in that thread

cards.diffense_note: This is literally diffense's own feedback-loop path (flag a card -> anchored comment -> task -> reply). The prototype validating the schema on the very gate code diffense will ride is a happy accident worth keeping in mind.

provenance.commit_headline: fix: poll GitHub PR review comments for @-mention triggers

lore.descriptive: New poller pass that reads inline PR review-line comments from /pulls/comments, filters for the mention, and emits pr-review-comment events carrying github_path, github_line, and branch_target. Called at the tail of _poll_mention_trigger.
lore.possibility: Inline diff-thread @-mentions now reach the gate at all; before this they were silently dropped because the gate only read /issues/comments.

invariants.name: exactly-once review-comment delivery
invariants.note: the seen-set is bounded; an evicted id whose comment is later edited can re-fire

zoom.summary: polls /pulls/comments for the mention; emits pr-review-comment events with path+line
per comment: skip if seen / no mention / self-author; extract pr_number from pull_request_url; attach branch_target via _fetch_pr_head_branch; then check parent reviews for summary mentions

zoom.note: this whole function is +new (file github.py deleted; package is new)

demo.content:
# reviewer comments on a diff line, mentioning @brr
POST /repos/Gurio/brr/pulls/comments  (reviewer)
# gate poll, next cycle:
GET  /repos/Gurio/brr/pulls/comments?since=... -> 200
  -> event: {github_kind: pr-review-comment, github_path: 'src/x.py',
             github_line: 42, github_pr_number: 64, branch_target: 'feature/x'}

provenance.commit_headline: fix: poll GitHub PR review comments for @-mention triggers

lore.descriptive: The transport gains an optional etag_store: when present it sends If-None-Match using the cached ETag for (method, path), returns (None, headers) on 304 without raising, and refreshes the store on a 2xx. Pollers thread cursor['etags'] through it.
lore.possibility: Steady-state polling on a quiet repo costs roughly zero REST budget: GitHub does not count conditional 304s against the rate limit. A stale ETag self-heals at the cost of one 200.

stats.signature_delta: + keyword-only etag_store: dict[str,str] | None on _request and _api_get
stats.behaviour: 304 -> (None, headers), no raise; 2xx with ETag -> store updated in place

invariants.name: poll-result correctness under caching
invariants.note: 304 means unchanged; a stale ETag self-heals at the cost of one 200, never a missed event

zoom.summary: cache_key = '{method} {path}'; since-cursor deliberately excluded from the key so the latest ETag still rides every poll

demo.content:
# first poll caches the ETag
GET /issues/comments            -> 200  ETag: "abc"   (rate -1)
# next poll, nothing changed
GET /issues/comments  If-None-Match: "abc"  -> 304   (rate  0)

cards._authoring_note: The design doc's worked-example card #1 attributed this to a 'cache.get_with_etag' symbol that does not exist; the real logic is here in client._request. Caught by grounding against the repo.

lore.descriptive: Delivery routes a pr-review-comment response to /pulls/{n}/comments/{cid}/replies (in-thread) instead of /issues/{n}/comments (top-level), and prepends a quote-pointer preface for all comment-origin kinds.
lore.possibility: A reply to an inline review comment lands in the diff thread where the reviewer asked, keeping the review conversation legible rather than scattering answers as orphaned top-level comments.

stats.routing: kind == pr-review-comment + comment_id + pr_number -> pull_comment_replies; else issue_comments
stats.quote_pointer: all kinds in _COMMENT_KINDS get a blockquote preface

invariants.name: the reply lands where the reviewer asked
invariants.note: routing keyed on kind + comment_id + pr_number; never an orphaned top-level comment

zoom.summary: pr-review-comment -> in-thread reply; others -> top-level comment; all quote-prefaced

cards._kind_status: PROMOTED — surfaced as a gap by this prototype; now a core kind in design-diffense.md. The open-taxonomy path (declare a custom kind -> raise a meta concern -> promote on recurrence) is exactly how it got here.

lore.descriptive: A single 1052-line github.py is deleted and split into a 12-module package (paths, cache, parse, client, state, wizard, polling, delivery, progress, loop, constants, __init__). The public surface is preserved verbatim in __init__.__all__.
lore.possibility: Separates a transport-agnostic, brnrd-reusable pure core (paths, cache, parse) from OSS-only transport (client, state, wizard, polling, delivery, progress, loop), so the managed brnrd backend can import the core without the sync requests stack — the seam codified in design-github-gate-vs-brnrd-app.md.

invariants.name: public import surface
invariants.note: __init__.__all__ preserves every exported name verbatim; the 1052-line move is internal-only

zoom.summary: monolith github.py -> github/ package; public surface unchanged
split axis is reuse: pure core (paths/cache/parse) vs OSS-only transport; __all__ pins the surface so internal refactors stay private

zoom.ref: the 12 module files at their locators

provenance.commit_headline: refactor(gates/github): split monolithic gate into a package

lore.descriptive: New boundary doc (227 lines, Status: accepted 2026-05-27) codifying the OSS gate vs managed brnrd GitHub App split: what each side owns, and which modules (paths/cache/parse) ship in brr.gates.github because brnrd imports them.
lore.possibility: The code seam between OSS and managed is now a citable contract rather than tribal knowledge; the managed-gates plan can lean on the package structure instead of re-implementing it.

stats.sibling_sync: design-git-layer-rework + plan-managed-gates-launch updated to point here

kb_axes.claim_delta: Asserts a citable OSS-gate ⟷ managed-brnrd boundary: paths/cache/parse are the shared pure core brnrd imports; client/state/wizard/polling/delivery/progress/loop are OSS-only transport. Previously tribal knowledge.

zoom.summary: new accepted boundary doc: OSS gate vs brnrd App, and the shared pure core
sections: what OSS owns / what brnrd owns / what both share (paths,cache,parse) / why both survive / deferred follow-ups

lore.descriptive: When the server answers 304 Not Modified to a conditional GET, _request returns (None, headers) without raising and leaves the cached ETag in place — so the next poll stays conditional and the 304 stays free.

stats.assertion_shape: payload is None; etag_store still holds the prior ETag
stats.fixtures: shares _FakeGitHubResponse with the request/etag test cluster
